rulings.Case Title: VICTOR TUNG VS. AMI QI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Wendy Lo And For Sanctions [Per Order Shortening Time]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, October 26, 2015, line 1. PLAINTIFF VICTOR TUNG's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Wendy Lo And For Sanctions GRANTED. Lo to appear for a 2-day, 14-hour deposition on dates mutually agreeable to all counsel no later than November 30, 2015. Sanctions granted in the amount of $890. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS C.R. TAM VS. THOMAS W. TAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Plaintiff'S Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, October 26, 2015, line 3. DEFENDANT THOMAS W. TAM's Motion To Disqualify Plaintiff'S Counsel GRANTED and counsel's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel is therefore MOOT. It is undisputed that counsel accepted employment adverse to a former client. Also, defendant adduces evidence that he gave counsel material confidential information - evidence that counsel's declaration does not rebut. Defendant's request for fees and costs is DENIED.= (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL GALINDO et al VS. SECURITY TITLE INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Motion To Compel Depositions; Request For Monetary And Terminating Sactions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, October 26, 2015, line 8 Defendants Civic Center Hotel and Balwantsinh Thakor's Motion To Compel Depositions; Request For Monetary And Terminating Sactions GRANTED. The 50-plus plaintiffs in this case are current or former residents of the defendant SRO hotel, who plead habitability and safety claims. Defendants moved to compel depositions of 12 plaintiffs. Plaintiffs' counsel asserts that many of these 12 are now homeless and cannot be readily located to be deposed. The 12 plaintiffs shall appear for deposition at times and places agreed by the parties within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. Terminating sanctions are DENIED. The request for monetary sanctions of $2,000 as to each of the 12 plaintiffs is also GRANTED, payable within 45 days of this order's entry if that plaintiff does not appear for deposition. Alternatively, to avoid sanctions, the plaintiff may voluntarily dismiss his or her claims within the 45-day period. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CINDY ZHANG VS. GEORGE JEFFRIES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Judgment For Possession And For Damages
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, October 26, 2015, line 7. PLAINTIFF CINDY ZHANG's Motion To Enforce Judgment For Possession And For Damages OFF CALENDAR per request of the moving party. = (501/RBU)

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, October 26, 2015, line 5. DEFENDANT GREGORY STOKUM's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. Plaintiff and his counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B. Failure to do so will result in the motion continuance. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MYLA ABLOG VS. ROBIN BAKER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, October 26, 2015, line 6. DEFENDANT ROBIN BAKER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED without leave to amend. "60 Day Notice" is fatally defective as it fails to comply with SFRO 37.9(c). "3 Day Notice" is fatally defective as it fails to comply with CC 1946.1. No authority is cited for the proposition that two defective notices constitute one valid notice. = (501/RBU)
